nodes.label: ai wiki corpus experiment
nodes.description: Phase 1 empirical study at experiments/ai_wiki/. 50 Wikipedia AI articles + 3 GitHub READMEs (huggingface_transformers, langchain, pytorch). Purpose: measure whether injecting existing-graph context into the extraction prompt (preflight) reduces duplicate-id rate and orphan ratio, at what token cost. Comparison: cycle 1 (no preflight) vs cycle 2 (heuristic preflight via brain context). Drove three project decisions: keep the preflight optional, add `algorithm` to the reference node types, and replace heuristic preflight with agentic tool calls in Phase 2.

nodes.label: no preflight cycle baseline
nodes.description: Cycle 1 of the AI Wiki experiment: 10 docs extracted without any graph context in the prompt. Result: 305 nodes, 314 rels, related_to ratio 0.6%, orphan ratio 22.3%, cost $1.0952, wall clock 673.8s. Baseline against which preflight is compared.

nodes.label: with preflight cycle treatment
nodes.description: Cycle 2 of the AI Wiki experiment: same 10 docs with a heuristic preflight pass (skim + brain context) added before extraction. Result: 288 nodes (-5.6%), 308 rels (-1.9%), related_to ratio 0.0% (vs 0.6%), orphan ratio 17.4% (vs 22.3%), cost $1.4234 (+$0.33), wall clock 946.2s (+272s). Quality improved on every dimension; cost rose by ~30%.

nodes.label: preflight consolidates nodes
nodes.description: Cycle 2 produced 5.6% fewer nodes than cycle 1 on the same corpus. Mechanism: when extracting a new document, the LLM saw existing ids (e.g. `artificial_neural_network`) and reused them instead of minting near-duplicates (e.g. `neural_network`, `artificial_neural_network`). Demonstrates that anti-duplicate consolidation is real and measurable.

nodes.label: preflight eliminates related to
nodes.description: Cycle 2 had 0 `related_to` edges; cycle 1 had 2 (0.6%). Mechanism speculation: seeing existing causal edges in the preflight context primes the model to use the same precise vocabulary instead of falling back to the vague label. Small absolute effect but consistent with the goal of keeping the fallback ratio under 5%.

nodes.label: preflight reduces orphans
nodes.description: Cycle 2 had 17.4% orphan ratio; cycle 1 had 22.3%. Both still above the 10% audit threshold but the trend is positive. Existing-graph context lets the model identify connection opportunities it would otherwise miss in isolation.

nodes.label: preflight cost premium
nodes.description: Cycle 2 cost $1.4234 vs cycle 1 $1.0952 — about +30% per corpus. Of that, $0.30 was the preflight skim itself and $0.03 was the extraction-pass overhead from the longer prompt. The preflight cost grows roughly linearly with corpus size; the consolidation benefit may not grow linearly. This cost asymmetry motivated the shift to agentic on-demand retrieval (Phase 2).

nodes.label: heuristic preflight replaced by tool calls
nodes.description: Phase 2 decision derived from the experiment: replace the always-on heuristic preflight by per-concept agentic tool calls (query_graph) issued during extraction. Rationale: tool calls retrieve ONLY the neighborhood of the concept being processed, not a global skim; cost scales with extraction needs, not corpus size. Preserves the consolidation benefit without the linear cost.

nodes.label: algorithm node type addition
nodes.description: Outcome of the experiments: the reference node types list was extended with `algorithm` after extension_requests.jsonl logged 14 attempts to extract nodes of type `algorithm` (backpropagation, k-means, etc.) during the AI Wiki corpus. The change took the reference list from 10 to 11 types and is now visible in lib/validate.REFERENCE_NODE_TYPES.

nodes.label: extract py script
nodes.description: experiments/ai_wiki/scripts/extract.py — single-document extraction harness. Uses anthropic.Anthropic with the claude-sonnet-4-6 model. Exposes a `query_graph` tool whose handler calls lib.query.context_for_topic. Tracks input/output tokens, cache hits, and per-doc cost. The first 'real' implementation of agentic extraction; the experiments run it against the corpus and ingest the JSON it returns.

nodes.label: run cycle py script
nodes.description: experiments/ai_wiki/scripts/run_cycle.py — orchestrates one full extract-and-ingest pass over the corpus in a chosen mode (no_preflight / with_preflight). Wipes the DB before starting (a clean comparison requires identical starting state). After the loop, exports the graph, runs audit, writes cycle_summary.json + stats.json + audit_report.txt to a per-cycle output directory. The 'experiment runner'.

nodes.label: ingest agentic py script
nodes.description: experiments/ai_wiki/scripts/ingest_agentic.py — variant of run_cycle that emphasises the agentic-mode test. Each document is extracted via tool calls and ingested before the next document's extraction runs, so subsequent documents see the live graph state through query_graph. Logs tool_calls per doc, total cost, total wall-clock. Designed for small adjacent-concept pairs (neural_network + backpropagation, transformer + attention, gradient_descent + backpropagation) to observe id-convergence directly.

nodes.label: make report py script
nodes.description: experiments/ai_wiki/scripts/make_report.py — diff-renderer comparing two cycle output directories. Reads cycle_summary.json + stats.json + graph_export.json from each side, computes headline metrics (totals, ratios, cost), node/rel type distributions, top-degree nodes, and per-concept neighborhood comparisons for a configurable watchlist. Produces the mini_comparison_report.md surfaced as the canonical experiment write-up.

nodes.label: db wipe pattern
nodes.description: _wipe_db helper used in the experiment scripts: shutil.rmtree if directory, unlink if file, plus globbing for Kuzu sidecar files `<db>.wal`, `<db>.shadow` etc. Necessary because Kuzu's persistent state is not a single file; a partial cleanup would leave a corrupted DB on the next open. Used only by experiments — production CLI never wipes.

nodes.label: api key file convention
nodes.description: experiments load the Anthropic API key from Important/API.txt at the project root. The Important/ directory is git-ignored (in .gitignore). load_api_key sys.exits with a clear message if the file is missing or empty. Choice: a single file path is simpler than env-var management and easier to rotate without touching shell rc files.

nodes.label: claude sonnet 4 6 model choice
nodes.description: MODEL constant = 'claude-sonnet-4-6' in extract.py. Sonnet, not Opus: extraction quality is good enough on Sonnet for structured outputs and the cost is roughly 5x lower. Opus would have made the cycle 1 vs 2 cost gap proportionally larger.

nodes.label: agentic convergence pairs
nodes.description: ingest_agentic.py's docstring identifies the tightly-coupled pairs used to test convergence: neural_network + backpropagation (many shared concepts), transformer + attention (same architecture cluster), gradient_descent + backpropagation (optimization + training loop). Pair-based testing isolates whether shared concepts converge to a single id when the second document runs.

nodes.label: extraction logs jsonl
nodes.description: results/<cycle>/extraction_logs.jsonl — one line per document with success/error, cost breakdown (input/output/cache_read/cache_write tokens), duration, parse retries, tool_calls. Lets the report script compute aggregate metrics and post-hoc filter outliers.

nodes.label: orphan ratio above threshold open issue
nodes.description: Both cycle 1 (22.3%) and cycle 2 (17.4%) ended with orphan ratios above the audit threshold (10%). The Wikipedia corpus stresses the relation pass — many introduced concepts are mentioned but not causally linked in the source. This is a CORPUS property, not a brAIn bug, but it motivates the SKILL.md emphasis on the explicit orphan review step.

rels.evidence: Cycle 1 of the AI Wiki experiment.
Cycle 2 of the AI Wiki experiment.
The two cycles test mutually exclusive extraction strategies on the same corpus.
Observed 5.6% fewer nodes when preflight context was injected.
related_to dropped from 0.6% to 0.0% — small absolute effect, suggestive but not definitive.
Orphan ratio dropped from 22.3% to 17.4%.
Cost rose by $0.33 (+30%) due to the preflight skim and longer extraction prompt.
Linear cost growth motivated the switch to agentic on-demand retrieval that scales with extraction need, not corpus size.
Phase 2 introduces query_graph as the on-demand replacement for the preflight skim.
Tool-call retrieval IS the on-demand-retrieval principle made operational for extraction.
14 algorithm-type rejections in extension_requests.jsonl drove the addition of algorithm to REFERENCE_NODE_TYPES.
The list grew from 10 to 11 reference types.
extract.py is the per-document extraction harness used by every cycle.
run_cycle.py orchestrates a full cycle over the corpus.
Agentic-mode variant for adjacent-concept pair testing.
Renders the cycle 1 vs cycle 2 comparison report.
_wipe_db helper called at the top of each cycle.
Sidecar-file cleanup is a Kuzu-specific concern; SQLite or Postgres would not need this.
load_api_key reads Important/API.txt at the start of every run.
MODEL constant set to claude-sonnet-4-6.
Choosing Sonnet over Opus contained the absolute cost gap to ~$0.33 — Opus would have made the gap proportionally larger.
Pair list documented in the script docstring.
Report script aggregates per-doc records to produce cost and duration totals.
Both cycles ended above the 10% audit threshold; treated as a corpus property, not a bug.
The persistent orphan rate is why SKILL.md emphasises the explicit orphan-review step.
The AI Wiki study is the concrete instance of the planned Phase 1 work.
